demographics.living_situation: lives at home with parents; school-aged

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help, they cannot stop vomiting, their stomach hurts constantly, and they are breathing extremely fast and deep like they just ran a marathon. I don't know what's wrong with them.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's the middle of the night and my child has been vomiting again and again, can't keep any water down, and now the breathing is really fast and heavy and they're barely able to stay awake.

symptom_narrative.onset: over the last three weeks, getting steadily worse; then a sudden change this morning
symptom_narrative.duration: three weeks of thirst and tiredness, and about the last 48 hours much worse, then vomiting and heavy breathing since this morning
symptom_narrative.character: constant vomiting, a stomach ache all over, and breathing that is very fast and deep; over the weeks, drinking huge amounts and passing urine constantly
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst they have ever been — can't get out of bed, can't keep anything down, and looks really unwell
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the stomach pain is all over, not in one spot
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the thirst, weeing and tiredness built up slowly over weeks; the vomiting, stomach pain and heavy breathing came on fast today
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has helped; they can't keep water down
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps getting worse on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: drinking a huge amount of water and juice, weeing all the time including waking overnight to wee, lost a lot of weight even though they were eating more, blurred vision today, very drowsy and floppy, and their breath smells oddly sweet
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before — has always been well
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound, too weak and unwell to attend school for days, can't keep fluids down

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: generally healthy, no ongoing medical conditions that we know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: school-aged child living at home; parent is present and made the call. The thirst was at first put down to the hot weather.

objective_data_offered.value: breathing is very fast, deep and heavy, 'like they just ran a marathon', even lying still in bed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent's description of the breathing pattern, not a measured respiratory rate

objective_data_offered.value: looks very unwell and drowsy, eyes look sunken, very floppy and hard to keep awake
their breath smells oddly sweet, almost like nail-polish or fruit
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective smell noticed by the parent

objective_data_offered.value: multiple empty water and juice bottles all around the bed from the constant drinking over recent days
has lost about 5 kg recently without trying, despite eating more than usual
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: estimated by the parent, not a scale reading at presentation
